title: orobanche major subsp. ritro
intro: Orobanche major subsp. ritro is a parasitic plant species that belongs to the family Orobanchaceae, commonly known as the broomrape family. It is a perennial plant that grows up to 70 cm in height, with a yellow or white inflorescence and a greenish-yellow or yellowish-white flower. The medicinal properties of Orobanche major subsp. ritro lie in its ability to exhibit anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities, which are believed to contribute to its potential health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and exhibiting immunomodulatory effects. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or extracts, and it is also used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, digestive issues, and respiratory problems.
